headline: Open-weight image and coding models surged while a new cluster of data-resilience techniques entered the field.
body: Local-inference tooling led the month: Krea 2 arrived as an open-source, uncensored image generator with ComfyUI and LoRA support, and GLM-5.2 surfaced as a free open-weight coder offering roughly 200 requests per five-hour window. Stereo vision, counterfactual reasoning, power iteration, and image inpainting all posted top momentum, while generative AI, vector database, and encoder-decoder improved on rising adoption, reinforced by DiffusionBench's push toward holistic diffusion transformer evaluation. A fresh breakout cluster appeared around data backup, immutability, disaster recovery, and ransomware protection, alongside new agentic-framework entrants including the doer-judge framework, emergent behavior, and role confusion. Counter to the momentum, tech stocks slumped on AI bubble fears, and Broward School Board paused an educator AI rollout over privacy and security concerns.

cited_items.title: GLM 5.2 FULLY FREE & FAST CODER: This AI Coder is CRAZY & WORKS!
New top local AI image generator is here! Already uncensored
DiffusionBench: Towards Holistic Evaluation of Generative Diffusion Transformers
Tech stocks slump as AI bubble fears loom
Broward School Board pauses rollout of AI platform for educators amid privacy and security concerns - CBS News
